According to Section 1003.3.1, Headroom, there must be a minimum ceiling height provided with at least an 80 inches (2032 mm) vertical clearance above any walking surface within a means of egress. Not more than 50 percent of the ceiling area shall have this headroom reduced by protruding objects.

According to Section 1005.3.2 of the Florida Building Code (FBC) as cited for the year 2023: For facilities equipped with an automatic sprinkler system and emergency voice/alarm communication systems, in buildings other than Group H and I-2 occupancies, the means-of-egress capacity factor per occupant is 0.15 inch (3.8 mm) for components other than stairways.
